edge_hypothesis: The ema_rsi_atr template has 3 independent filters: EMA_9>EMA_21 (trend), RSI in band (confirmation), ATR>20MA (volatility). Previous specs kept RSI bands tight (40-70), making RSI the binding constraint. By widening RSI to near-permissive levels (25-85 long, 15-75 short), we transform this into a VOLATILITY EXPANSION detector with basic directional confirmation. The hypothesis: ATR exceeding its 20-period mean signals a volatility regime shift that precedes large directional moves. When ATR expands AND EMAs align, the market is transitioning from compression to trend — a high-conviction entry. ETH/1h ranging PF=1.115 for library_augmented (ema_rsi_atr template) suggests the ATR gate captures mean-reversion opportunities even in ranging markets. Wide exits (3.0-3.5x TP) capitalize on the large moves that follow volatility expansion. Regime assumption: TRENDING (volatility expansion + EMA alignment = trend initiation). Invalidation: if PF < 0.95 with 100+ trades, ATR expansion is not a reliable standalone signal and the RSI filter was providing essential noise reduction.

variants.name: atr_expansion_wide_rsi
variants.description: EMA_RSI_ATR with RSI bands widened to 25-85 (long) and 15-75 (short), effectively making RSI permissive. The ATR>20MA volatility gate becomes the dominant entry filter. This tests whether ATR expansion itself has predictive value for trend continuation. Standard 2:1 R:R with safe stops.
entry_long: EMA_9 > EMA_21 confirms short-term uptrend alignment.
ATR_14 > 20-period rolling mean of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
entry_short: EMA_9 < EMA_21 confirms short-term downtrend alignment.
ATR_14 > 20-period rolling mean of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: permissive RSI may admit low-quality signals in choppy ATR expansion events
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ema_rsi_atr volatility gate — ATR>20MA is primary signal

variants.name: atr_expansion_fee_killer
variants.description: Ultra-wide exits (5:1 TP/stop ratio) to maximize profit per trade and minimize fee drag. The thesis: if ATR expansion truly signals the start of a large move, then holding for 5.0x ATR TP captures the full move. This is the highest-conviction variant — fewer wins but each win is massive. Tests whether ATR expansion signals produce large-enough moves to justify extreme R:R.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 5.0x TP may rarely be hit, producing extremely low win rate and long drawdown periods
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

variants.name: atr_expansion_moderate_control
variants.description: Control variant with slightly tighter RSI (35-75) to measure the marginal value of RSI filtering. If this outperforms the wide_rsi variant, RSI adds signal value even in an ATR-dominant strategy. If it underperforms, RSI is pure noise reduction that blocks valid entries. Standard exits (2:1 R:R) for consistent comparison.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 35 and 75 (moderate RSI filter retained).
entry_short: RSI_14 between 25 and 65 (moderate RSI filter retained).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 4h and 1h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: moderate RSI may block valid ATR expansion signals in strong trends (RSI stays high)
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ema_rsi_atr volatility gate — ATR>20MA primary signal
